programmes.show_description: Alf has a fight at a tea dance and arranges to meet up again with his adversary outside his house. S3 E5
With Boomtown Rats and Suzi Quatro. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon helps subsidise a man's dream of adventure and excitement, but the dream becomes a nightmare until a woman's devotion brings the man back to happiness. S1 E6: The Pearls of Peace

Nothing before or since has equalled the splendour and magnificence of Tutankhamun but is there a darker secret held within. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Giant aircraft carriers dominated the Pacific War between Japan and the USA - Pearl Harbor was launched to destroy US carriers but failed. Ep4: Aircraft Carriers
Priceless art, personal assets and national treasures were an extraordinary amount of loot prized by Hitler as much as territory - we discover the organised plunder of these riches. S1 E5: Nazi Gold
After escaping the German invasion of their homeland in 1939, Polish fighter pilots and ground forces played a key role in the liberation of western Europe. Ep5: The Free Polish Forces
The definitive combat helicopter saw first blood in South East Asia and has been transport, gunship, smoke ship, command centre, MediVac and dozens of other roles. S2 E5
We experience the event that changed the war's course and feel the momentum behind the attack that provoked the world's greatest superpower to join the Allied forces. Ep4: Pearl Harbor
We tell the story of the crucial battles for control of the Mediterranean, North Africa, and the irreplaceable oil fields in World War 2 and the story of the war in Greece.
Examining the history of war tactics. We explore defensive battles, including the 1943 Battle of Kursk where the Red Army dug in against the Germans and triumphed. S1 E11
An exploration of London's underground railway and skyline, the origins of Metro-land - London's vast suburbia - and the clever marketing that tempted millions to move outside the city. Ep4
Artillery hasn't really changed; once the foundation of battle in WWII, it appears as though artillery isn't going anywhere until political and moral issues regarding accuracy arise. E5: Gods of War
Divers explore underwater graveyards from the American bombardment of the Japanese fleet in WWII and how the German liner Wilhelm Gustloff was torpedoed by a Russian sub. Ep5: Savage Revenge
As Britain began to strike Nazi Germany, it was soon suffering losses from hi-tech air defences; in 1941, British paratroops were sent to seize a German radar system which needed to be cracked.
There is an accident at the Lewis Bishop Ship Yard and one of the fitters is badly hurt. S2 E10: A Wreath with Our Names On
Arthur needs to raise some cash fast, but will insurance fraud and pawning the jewellery of 'er indoors' raise more problems than it will solve? S10 E1: A Fridge Too Far
Ross and Demelza look to forget the past and rebuild their marriage, but new arrivals threaten their fragile reconciliation. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E1
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. Sam gets to work on proving DCI Hunt's innocence in the shooting of a local underworld bookie. Starring John Simm. S2 E7
Alf complains to the DHSS that he should have been paid more winter's heating allowance. He has a loud revealing dream in front of his friends. S3 E6
With Cliff Richard and Dave Edmunds and Rockville. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
A slimy gossip columnist is killed and it's up to Simon to find the true murderer from the list of subjects. S1 E7: The Arrow of God

After WWII, Britain rushed the Comet jetliner into service. Mysterious crashes led to tests that reshaped aircraft design forever. Narrated by Robert Powell.
The Allied fronts in both western and eastern Europe had become faster-moving by 1944 - a new form of aircraft was needed to deliver swift powerful attacks against German lines. Ep5: Fighter Bombers
We take a look at one of WWII's most significant theatres of warfare, in which Germany's U-boat fleet relentlessly attacked Britain's supply lines. S1 E6: Battle of the Atlantic
Despite the general inexperience of its young pilots, the Royal Air Force Fighter Command ultimately prevailed in its battle against the German Luftwaffe. Ep6: RAF Fighter Command
The F-15 Eagle immediately turned the tide of the first Gulf War to prove its greatness, and was involved in one of the most bizarre combat incidents during that conflict. S2 E6
Devastation touches the civilians of Germany; with the US as a newly-engaged ally, Western forces bring the war to the Axis front but at extreme costs. Ep5: Bombing The Reich
The campaign in Tunisia in World War Two saw the battles that finally ended the brutal war in the desert. We look at the weapons used and tactics and tell the story of the battle itself.
Examining the history of war tactics. We explore the history and principles of guerrilla warfare. S1 E12
The story of the boffins and experimenters who developed the first workable radar system in time to help defeat the Nazi attacks in the Battle of Britain. Ep1: The Men Who Invented Radar

programmes.show_name: Margaret Thatcher: The Lady's Not for Turning
programmes.show_description: We examine the life and career of Margaret Thatcher, Britain's first female Prime Minister and one of the dominant political figures of 20th-century Britain.
...Where Were You? There are some news events that change the world. In today's episode we take you back to July 25th 2000 and the fatal crash of the Air France Concorde flight 4590.
Two of the greatest failed invasions in the history of naval warfare - how the Spanish Armada was defeated and why the Allied fleet was crushed in WWI - are explored. Ep6: Failed Invasions
Ordered to attack the St Nazaire U-boat base, British commandos undertook 'the greatest raid of all' by turning an old destroyer into a live bomb to ram the gates. Ep4: Storm at St. Nazaire
Jack is in demand to speak up for Geordie Watson, the local Labour candidate in a forthcoming election. S2 E11: The Way It Was in Murmansk
When Arthur, Dave and Ray take a trip across the English Channel to take advantage of duty-free prices, it's Arthur who ultimately gets taken advantage of. S10 E2: Another Case of Van Blank
As Elizabeth becomes more ambivalent about Valentine, George courts Sir Francis Basset for the vacant magistracy position. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E2
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. When CID hear of a dead miner, all roads lead to the notorious cop killer Leslie Johns. Starring John Simm. S2 E8
